channel.title: EMERGENZ Biosecurity Intelligence Dashboard
channel.description: High-severity biosecurity signals and recent authority/media coverage. Generated 2026-05-28T20:32:32.832Z.

item.title: [ACTION] Ebola disease — Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo
item.description: WHO has declared the Bundibugyo ebolavirus outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with Africa CDC confirming active outbreak monitoring in both the DRC and Uganda. Multi-health-zone spread in eastern DRC continues; cross-border movement elevates geographic risk. Specific case and fatality counts require direct verification against the current WHO Disease Outbreak News (who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news) or Africa CDC epidemic intelligence report.

item.title: [CONCERN] Andes virus hantavirus cluster — MV Hondius travel-linked
item.description: Andes hantavirus cluster linked to a South America–Antarctica expedition cruise aboard MV Hondius. 11 reported cases (9 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths across multiple countries, with multinational case-finding and post-exposure monitoring continuing.

item.title: [CONCERN] Measles — United States
item.description: CDC confirmed 1,952 measles cases across 40 jurisdictions in 2026 (as of May 21), representing 29 outbreaks with 93% of cases outbreak-associated. No deaths reported in 2026. U.S. kindergartener MMR coverage has declined to 92.5% (from 95.2% in 2019–2020), leaving approximately 286,000 kindergarteners unvaccinated — the structural driver of sustained outbreak activity.

item.title: [CONCERN] Mpox — clade I, Africa & travel-linked importations
item.description: Mpox clade I and travel-linked importation monitoring remain operationally significant for clinical recognition, specimen routing, and laboratory clade differentiation. Verify current country-level trends and any importation details against specific Africa CDC, WHO, or CDC updates before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Cholera — sub-Saharan Africa, including Republic of Congo
item.description: Africa CDC reported 40,707 cholera cases continent-wide from January 1 through May 14, 2026 — a 65% year-over-year decline compared to the same period in 2025. Despite the overall volume decrease, case fatality ratios remain elevated in conflict-affected and low-healthcare-access settings. Sudan, Ethiopia, Somalia, Mozambique, Malawi, and Zimbabwe continue reporting active transmission.

item.title: [CONCERN] Chikungunya — global outbreak activity
item.description: Chikungunya surveillance remains active across parts of the Americas and other mosquito-active regions. Verify current country burden and case totals against specific PAHO or WHO updates before publication.

item.title: Google News: U.S. Says Deal Reached With Kenya on Ebola Quarantine Facility for Americans - WSJ
item.description: U.S. Says Deal Reached With Kenya on Ebola Quarantine Facility for Americans WSJ

item.title: Google News: Makeshift hospital for Americans exposed to Ebola will open in Kenya on Friday - The Washington Post
item.description: Makeshift hospital for Americans exposed to Ebola will open in Kenya on Friday The Washington Post

item.title: ABC News: No one in courtroom to speak on behalf of victims of man who killed 4 sleeping homeless men
item.description: A man convicted of bludgeoning four men to death with a metal bar as they slept on the New York City streets has been sentenced to 40 years to life in prison

item.title: Google News: Canada, U.S., Mexico announce Ebola travel measures for FIFA World Cup - CBC
item.description: Canada, U.S., Mexico announce Ebola travel measures for FIFA World Cup CBC

item.title: Google News: As Ebola outbreak in Central Africa grows, the U.S. turns itself into a fortress - statnews.com
item.description: As Ebola outbreak in Central Africa grows, the U.S. turns itself into a fortress statnews.com

item.title: NBC News: Americans who get Ebola will go to Europe for treatment, not U.S., officials say
item.description: If additional Americans contract Ebola and need medical care, they will be sent to Europe rather than brought to home the U.S., senior administration officials said.

item.title: Google News: Americans who get Ebola will go to Europe for treatment, not U.S., officials say - NBC News
item.description: Americans who get Ebola will go to Europe for treatment, not U.S., officials say NBC News

item.title: STAT News: STAT+: As Ebola outbreak in Central Africa grows, the U.S. turns itself into a fortress
item.description: The U.S. is taking extraordinary steps to keep Ebola out. But its policies may create risks for Americans infected overseas and discourage volunteers from responding to the outbreak.

item.title: Google News: Quarantine Unit for Americans Exposed to Ebola to Open in Kenya Friday - The New York Times
item.description: Quarantine Unit for Americans Exposed to Ebola to Open in Kenya Friday The New York Times

item.title: Google News: WHO chief heads to ‘hardest hit’ Ituri as DRC’s 17th Ebola outbreak spreads - Al Jazeera
item.description: WHO chief heads to ‘hardest hit’ Ituri as DRC’s 17th Ebola outbreak spreads Al Jazeera

item.title: Google News: FIU experts answer questions about the Ebola outbreak in the DRC - Florida International University
item.description: FIU experts answer questions about the Ebola outbreak in the DRC Florida International University

item.title: NBC News: FDA panel recommends updating covid shots to target XFG strain
item.description: A Food and Drug Administration advisory panel on Thursday recommended updating this fall’s Covid shots to target the XFG variant, a fast-growing strain nicknamed “stratus.”The recommendation — from the FDA’s Vaccines and Related Biological Products Advisory Committee — is...

item.title: Google News: Florida measles cases reaching historic levels - The Palm Beach Post
item.description: Florida measles cases reaching historic levels The Palm Beach Post

item.title: Google News: WHO urges Ebola treatments, vaccines be tested only in trials amid Bundibugyo outbreak - Reuters
item.description: WHO urges Ebola treatments, vaccines be tested only in trials amid Bundibugyo outbreak Reuters

item.description: Experts convened by WHO advise on candidate treatments and vaccines for Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus World Health Organization (WHO)

item.title: STAT News: STAT+: Biotech veteran Jeremy Levin on why the industry’s future is secure, but American leadership is at risk
item.description: Biotech is producing scientific breakthroughs that once seemed impossible. But according to longtime industry executive Jeremy Levin, the institutions that support these advances, from regulators, to investors, and even public…

item.title: Google News: Americans exposed to Ebola won't immediately return to U.S. - Axios
item.description: Americans exposed to Ebola won't immediately return to U.S. Axios
